Figure 2. Atf3 is indispensable for IL-4-induced macrophage fusion. 1026 (A) Schematic representation of the IL-4 stimulation protocol to generate MGCs (a). Cells expressing 1027 nuclear H2B-EGFP (yellow) were mixed with cells expressing membrane-targeted mTmG-TdTOMATO 1028 (cyan). Both acytokinetic cell division (b) and cell-cell fusion (c) drive multinucleation. 1029 (B) Bright field single frames from time-lapse live imaging of control BMDMs stimulated with IL-4 for 1030 three days. Both cell-cell fusion (asterisks) and incomplete cytokinesis (rectangle) drive multinucleation. 1031

Figure 5. 25-HC-mediated inhibition of the mevalonate pathway and protein prenylation recapitulates 1148 the phenotypes of Atf3 loss. 1149 (A) Schematic representation of cholesterol and isoprenoid metabolism in the cell, highlighting the 1150 mevalonate pathway. The ER-resident enzyme Cholesterol-25-hydroxylase (Ch25h) generates the 1151 oxysterol 25-Hydroxycholesterol (25-HC). 25-HC causes sequestration of a free cholesterol pool from the 1152 plasma membrane (a). 25-HC regulates cholesterol biosynthesis by suppressing the rate-limiting enzyme 1153 of the mevalonate pathway Hmgcr (b) and by binding the Insulin-induced gene (INSIG) and forming a 1154 complex that retains Sreb2-Scap inactive in the ER (c). In addition, 25-HC binds LXR-RXR transcription 1155 factor dimers to initiate expression of genes involved in the absorption, degradation, transportation, 1156 and excretion of cholesterol (d). Besides de novo synthesis of cholesterol, the mevalonate pathway also 1157 generates isoprenoids. Isoprenoid intermediates, farnesyl pyrophosphate and geranylgeranyl 1158 pyrophosphate, are used for prenylation of various proteins. Compounds inhibiting the mevalonate 1159 pathway (25-HC, Statins) and protein prenylation (GGTI-2133, Lonafarnib) are depicted. The model was 1160 generated using BioRender.com. 1161 (B) Heatmap shows differential expression of selected genes associated with cholesterol biosynthesis in 1162 control and Atf3
